{"tests":[{"filename":"001_h_command_line_symlink_dir.sh","body":"#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
tmpdir=$(mktemp -d)
trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT
mkdir -p "$tmpdir/target"
: > "$tmpdir/target/file"
ln -s "$tmpdir/target" "$tmpdir/linkdir"
out=$("$UTIL" -H "$tmpdir/linkdir" -maxdepth 1 -type f -printf '%f\n' | sort)
expected='file'
if [[ "$out" != "$expected" ]]; then echo "-H did not examine command-line symlink directory" >&2; exit 1; fi
","exercises":"-H follows symlink starting points to directories","expected":"child file under command-line symlink directory is found","expected_to_fail":false},{"filename":"002_l_follows_symlink_dir.sh","body":"#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
tmpdir=$(mktemp -d)
trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT
mkdir -p "$tmpdir/root" "$tmpdir/target"
: > "$tmpdir/target/child"
ln -s "$tmpdir/target" "$tmpdir/root/link"
out=$("$UTIL" -L "$tmpdir/root" -type f -printf '%P\n' | sort)
expected='link/child'
if [[ "$out" != "$expected" ]]; then echo "-L did not recurse into symlinked directory" >&2; exit 1; fi
","exercises":"-L follows symbolic links during traversal","expected":"regular file reachable only through symlinked directory is printed","expected_to_fail":false},{"filename":"003_p_reports_symlink_itself.sh","body":"#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
tmpdir=$(mktemp -d)
trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT
mkdir -p "$tmpdir/root"
: > "$tmpdir/target"
ln -s "$tmpdir/target" "$tmpdir/root/link"
out=$("$UTIL" -P "$tmpdir/root" -maxdepth 1 -type l -printf '%P\n' | sort)
expected='link'
if [[ "$out" != "$expected" ]]; then echo "-P did not test symlink itself" >&2; exit 1; fi
","exercises":"-P never follows symbolic links","expected":"symlink matches -type l","expected_to_fail":false},{"filename":"004_d_help_lists_debug_options.sh","body":"#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
tmpdir=$(mktemp -d)
trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT
out=$("$UTIL" -D help 2>&1)
if [[ "$out" != *"Valid arguments"* ]]; then echo "-D help did not list debug arguments" >&2; exit 1; fi
","exercises":"-D help debug option","expected":"debug help output names valid debug arguments","expected_to_fail":false},{"filename":"005_o3_preserves_query_result.sh","body":"#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
tmpdir=$(mktemp -d)
trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT
mkdir -p "$tmpdir/root/sub"
: > "$tmpdir/root/a.txt"
: > "$tmpdir/root/sub/b.txt"
: > "$tmpdir/root/c.log"
out=$("$UTIL" -O3 "$tmpdir/root" -name '*.txt' -type f -printf '%P\n' | sort)
expected=$'a.txt\nsub/b.txt'
if [[ "$out" != "$expected" ]]; then echo "-O3 changed matching result" >&2; exit 1; fi
","exercises":"-O3 query optimisation preserves effect","expected":"only .txt regular files are printed","expected_to_fail":false},{"filename":"006_default_expression_prints_startpoint.sh","body":"#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
tmpdir=$(mktemp -d)
trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT
: > "$tmpdir/file"
out=$("$UTIL" "$tmpdir/file")
expected="$tmpdir/file"
if [[ "$out" != "$expected" ]]; then echo "default expression was not -print" >&2; exit 1; fi
","exercises":"default expression when no expression is given","expected":"starting file path is printed","expected_to_fail":false},{"filename":"007_maxdepth_zero_only_startpoint.sh","body":"#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
tmpdir=$(mktemp -d)
trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT
mkdir -p "$tmpdir/root/sub"
: > "$tmpdir/root/sub/file"
out=$("$UTIL" "$tmpdir/root" -maxdepth 0 -printf '%p\n')
expected="$tmpdir/root"
if [[ "$out" != "$expected" ]]; then echo "-maxdepth 0 processed below starting point" >&2; exit 1; fi
","exercises":"-maxdepth 0","expected":"only the starting point is processed","expected_to_fail":false},{"filename":"008_mindepth_one_skips_startpoint.sh","body":"#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
tmpdir=$(mktemp -d)
trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT
mkdir -p "$tmpdir/root/child"
out=$("$UTIL" "$tmpdir/root" -mindepth 1 -maxdepth 1 -printf '%P\n' | sort)
expected='child'
if [[ "$out" != "$expected" ]]; then echo "-mindepth 1 did not skip starting point" >&2; exit 1; fi
","exercises":"-mindepth 1","expected":"depth-1 child is printed but starting point is not","expected_to_fail":false},{"filename":"009_depth_processes_contents_first.sh","body":"#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
tmpdir=$(mktemp -d)
trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT
mkdir -p "$tmpdir/root/sub"
: > "$tmpdir/root/sub/file"
out=$("$UTIL" "$tmpdir/root" -depth -printf '%P\n')
expected=$'sub/file\nsub'
if [[ "$out" != "$expected" ]]; then echo "-depth did not process contents before directories" >&2; exit 1; fi
","exercises":"-depth depth-first traversal","expected":"file is printed before containing directories","expected_to_fail":false},{"filename":"010_files0_from_reads_startpoints.sh","body":"#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
tmpdir=$(mktemp -d)
trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT
: > "$tmpdir/a"
: > "$tmpdir/b"
printf '%s\0%s\0' "$tmpdir/a" "$tmpdir/b" > "$tmpdir/list"
out=$("$UTIL" -files0-from "$tmpdir/list" -maxdepth 0 -printf '%f\n' | sort)
expected=$'a\nb'
if [[ "$out" != "$expected" ]]; then echo "-files0-from did not read NUL-separated start points" >&2; exit 1; fi
","exercises":"-files0-from with NUL-separated start points","expected":"both listed starting files are processed","expected_to_fail":false},{"filename":"011_files0_from_empty_name_errors.sh","body":"#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
tmpdir=$(mktemp -d)
trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT
printf '\0' > "$tmpdir/list"
set +e
"$UTIL" -files0-from "$tmpdir/list" -maxdepth 0 -print > "$tmpdir/out" 2> "$tmpdir/err"
status=$?
set -e
if [[ $status -eq 0 ]]; then echo "-files0-from accepted zero-length file name" >&2; exit 1; fi
","exercises":"-files0-from rejects zero-length starting point","expected":"find exits nonzero","expected_to_fail":true},{"filename":"012_files0_from_with_path_errors.sh","body":"#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
tmpdir=$(mktemp -d)
trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT
: > "$tmpdir/a"
printf '%s\0' "$tmpdir/a" > "$tmpdir/list"
set +e
"$UTIL" "$tmpdir/a" -files0-from "$tmpdir/list" -print > "$tmpdir/out" 2> "$tmpdir/err"
status=$?
set -e
if [[ $status -eq 0 ]]; then echo "-files0-from was allowed with command-line start point" >&2; exit 1; fi
","exercises":"-files0-from is mutually exclusive with command-line paths","expected":"find exits nonzero","expected_to_fail":true},{"filename":"013_missing_startpoint_errors.sh","body":"#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
tmpdir=$(mktemp -d)
trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT
set +e
"$UTIL" "$tmpdir/missing" -print > "$tmpdir/out" 2> "$tmpdir/err"
status=$?
set -e
if [[ $status -eq 0 ]]; then echo "missing starting point did not cause nonzero exit" >&2; exit 1; fi
","exercises":"nonexistent starting point error","expected":"find exits nonzero","expected_to_fail":true},{"filename":"014_follow_makes_lname_false.sh","body":"#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
tmpdir=$(mktemp -d)
trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT
mkdir -p "$tmpdir/root"
: > "$tmpdir/target"
ln -s "$tmpdir/target" "$tmpdir/root/link"
out=$("$UTIL" "$tmpdir/root" -follow -lname '*' -printf '%P\n')
expected=''
if [[ "$out" != "$expected" ]]; then echo "-follow did not make -lname false for resolved symlink" >&2; exit 1; fi
","exercises":"-follow positional option and -lname","expected":"resolved symlink is not matched by -lname","expected_to_fail":false},{"filename":"015_name_pattern_matches_leading_dot.sh","body":"#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
tmpdir=$(mktemp -d)
trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT
mkdir -p "$tmpdir/root"
: > "$tmpdir/root/.hidden.c"
: > "$tmpdir/root/plain.c"
: > "$tmpdir/root/plain.h"
out=$("$UTIL" "$tmpdir/root" -maxdepth 1 -name '*.c' -printf '%f\n' | sort)
expected=$'.hidden.c\nplain.c'
if [[ "$out" != "$expected" ]]; then echo "-name pattern did not match expected basenames" >&2; exit 1; fi
","exercises":"-name shell pattern matching","expected":"*.c matches both plain.c and .hidden.c","expected_to_fail":false},{"filename":"016_iname_case_insensitive.sh","body":"#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
tmpdir=$(mktemp -d)
trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT
mkdir -p "$tmpdir/root"
: > "$tmpdir/root/Foo"
: > "$tmpdir/root/foo"
: > "$tmpdir/root/bar"
out=$("$UTIL" "$tmpdir/root" -maxdepth 1 -iname 'foo' -printf '%f\n' | sort)
expected=$'Foo\nfoo'
if [[ "$out" != "$expected" ]]; then echo "-iname was not case-insensitive" >&2; exit 1; fi
","exercises":"-iname case-insensitive basename pattern","expected":"Foo and foo both match pattern foo","expected_to_fail":false},{"filename":"017_ipath_case_insensitive_whole_path.sh","body":"#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
tmpdir=$(mktemp -d)
trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT
mkdir -p "$tmpdir/root/sub"
: > "$tmpdir/root/sub/File"
pattern="$tmpdir/ROOT/SUB/FILE"
out=$("$UTIL" "$tmpdir/root" -ipath "$pattern" -printf '%P\n')
expected='sub/File'
if [[ "$out" != "$expected" ]]; then echo "-ipath did not match whole path case-insensitively" >&2; exit 1; fi
","exercises":"-ipath case-insensitive whole-path pattern","expected":"sub/File matches uppercase absolute path pattern","expected_to_fail":false},{"filename":"018_regextype_posix_extended.sh","body":"#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
tmpdir=$(mktemp -d)
trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT
mkdir -p "$tmpdir/root"
: > "$tmpdir/root/foo123"
: > "$tmpdir/root/fooX"
out=$("$UTIL" "$tmpdir/root" -regextype posix-extended -regex '.*/foo[0-9]+' -printf '%f\n')
expected='foo123'
if [[ "$out" != "$expected" ]]; then echo "-regextype posix-extended regex did not match expected file" >&2; exit 1; fi
","exercises":"-regextype posix-extended with -regex","expected":"only foo123 matches the POSIX extended whole-path regex","expected_to_fail":false},{"filename":"019_type_combined_list.sh","body":"#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
tmpdir=$(mktemp -d)
trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT
mkdir -p "$tmpdir/root/sub"
: > "$tmpdir/root/file"
: > "$tmpdir/target"
ln -s "$tmpdir/target" "$tmpdir/root/link"
out=$("$UTIL" "$tmpdir/root" -maxdepth 1 -type f,d,l -printf '%y:%P\n' | sort)
expected=$'d:\nd:sub\nf:file\nl:link'
if [[ "$out" != "$expected" ]]; then echo "combined -type list did not match files directories and symlinks" >&2; exit 1; fi
","exercises":"GNU -type f,d,l combined type list","expected":"regular file, directory, and symlink entries all match","expected_to_fail":false},{"filename":"020_empty_files_and_directories.sh","body":"#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
tmpdir=$(mktemp -d)
trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT
mkdir -p "$tmpdir/root/emptydir" "$tmpdir/root/nonemptydir"
: > "$tmpdir/root/emptyfile"
printf x > "$tmpdir/root/nonemptyfile"
: > "$tmpdir/root/nonemptydir/child"
out=$("$UTIL" "$tmpdir/root" -empty -printf '%P\n' | sort)
expected=$'emptydir\nemptyfile'
if [[ "$out" != "$expected" ]]; then echo "-empty did not match exactly empty files and directories" >&2; exit 1; fi
","exercises":"-empty test","expected":"empty regular file and empty directory match","expected_to_fail":false},{"filename":"021_size_bytes_exact.sh","body":"#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
tmpdir=$(mktemp -d)
trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT
mkdir -p "$tmpdir/root"
printf abc > "$tmpdir/root/three"
printf abcd > "$tmpdir/root/four"
: > "$tmpdir/root/zero"
out=$("$UTIL" "$tmpdir/root" -type f -size 3c -printf '%f\n')
expected='three'
if [[ "$out" != "$expected" ]]; then echo "-size 3c did not match exact byte size" >&2; exit 1; fi
","exercises":"-size n c byte units","expected":"only the three-byte file matches -size 3c","expected_to_fail":false},{"filename":"022_perm_all_bits.sh","body":"#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
tmpdir=$(mktemp -d)
trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT
mkdir -p "$tmpdir/root"
: > "$tmpdir/root/all"
: > "$tmpdir/root/extra"
: > "$tmpdir/root/no"
chmod 664 "$tmpdir/root/all"
chmod 775 "$tmpdir/root/extra"
chmod 644 "$tmpdir/root/no"
out=$("$UTIL" "$tmpdir/root" -type f -perm -664 -printf '%f\n' | sort)
expected=$'all\nextra'
if [[ "$out" != "$expected" ]]; then echo "-perm -mode did not require all requested bits" >&2; exit 1; fi
","exercises":"-perm -mode all permission bits set","expected":"files with all 664 bits, including extra bits, match","expected_to_fail":false},{"filename":"023_newer_reference_mtime.sh","body":"#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
tmpdir=$(mktemp -d)
trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT
mkdir -p "$tmpdir/root"
: > "$tmpdir/ref"
: > "$tmpdir/root/old"
: > "$tmpdir/root/new"
touch -d '2000-01-01 UTC' "$tmpdir/ref" "$tmpdir/root/old"
touch -d '2001-01-01 UTC' "$tmpdir/root/new"
out=$("$UTIL" "$tmpdir/root" -type f -newer "$tmpdir/ref" -printf '%f\n')
expected='new'
if [[ "$out" != "$expected" ]]; then echo "-newer did not compare modification time to reference" >&2; exit 1; fi
","exercises":"-newer reference modification time","expected":"only file newer than reference is printed","expected_to_fail":false},{"filename":"024_newermt_direct_time.sh","body":"#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
tmpdir=$(mktemp -d)
trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT
mkdir -p "$tmpdir/root"
: > "$tmpdir/root/old"
: > "$tmpdir/root/new"
touch -d '2000-01-01 UTC' "$tmpdir/root/old"
touch -d '2001-01-01 UTC' "$tmpdir/root/new"
out=$("$UTIL" "$tmpdir/root" -type f -newermt '2000-06-01 UTC' -printf '%f\n')
expected='new'
if [[ "$out" != "$expected" ]]; then echo "-newermt did not interpret direct time threshold" >&2; exit 1; fi
","exercises":"-newerXY with X=m Y=t via -newermt","expected":"only file modified after the direct time is printed","expected_to_fail":false},{"filename":"025_samefile_hard_link.sh","body":"#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
tmpdir=$(mktemp -d)
trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT
mkdir -p "$tmpdir/root"
printf data > "$tmpdir/root/original"
ln "$tmpdir/root/original" "$tmpdir/root/link"
: > "$tmpdir/root/other"
out=$("$UTIL" "$tmpdir/root" -samefile "$tmpdir/root/original" -printf '%f\n' | sort)
expected=$'link\noriginal'
if [[ "$out" != "$expected" ]]; then echo "-samefile did not match hard links to same inode" >&2; exit 1; fi
","exercises":"-samefile inode comparison","expected":"original and hard link both match","expected_to_fail":false},{"filename":"026_print0_outputs_nul_terminator.sh","body":"#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
tmpdir=$(mktemp -d)
trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT
file="$tmpdir/name with spaces"
: > "$file"
got=$("$UTIL" "$file" -maxdepth 0 -print0 | od -An -tx1 | tr -d ' \n')
expected=$(printf '%s\0' "$file" | od -An -tx1 | tr -d ' \n')
if [[ "$got" != "$expected" ]]; then echo "-print0 did not output exact NUL-terminated path" >&2; exit 1; fi
","exercises":"-print0 action","expected":"output bytes are path followed by ASCII NUL","expected_to_fail":false},{"filename":"027_printf_basename_and_size.sh","body":"#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
tmpdir=$(mktemp -d)
trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT
printf abcd > "$tmpdir/data"
out=$("$UTIL" "$tmpdir/data" -maxdepth 0 -printf '%f:%s')
expected='data:4'
if [[ "$out" != "$expected" ]]; then echo "-printf %f:%s produced unexpected output" >&2; exit 1; fi
","exercises":"-printf %f and %s directives without implicit newline","expected":"basename and byte size are printed exactly","expected_to_fail":false},{"filename":"028_fprint_truncates_and_writes_file.sh","body":"#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
tmpdir=$(mktemp -d)
trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT
: > "$tmpdir/input"
printf oldcontent > "$tmpdir/out"
"$UTIL" "$tmpdir/input" -maxdepth 0 -fprint "$tmpdir/out" > "$tmpdir/stdout"
out=$(cat "$tmpdir/out")
expected="$tmpdir/input"
if [[ "$out" != "$expected" ]]; then echo "-fprint did not truncate and write output file" >&2; exit 1; fi
","exercises":"-fprint action creates/truncates output file","expected":"output file contains the matched path","expected_to_fail":false},{"filename":"029_execdir_runs_in_file_directory.sh","body":"#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
tmpdir=$(mktemp -d)
trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT
mkdir -p "$tmpdir/root/sub"
: > "$tmpdir/root/sub/target"
PATH=/usr/bin:/bin "$UTIL" "$tmpdir/root" -type f -name target -execdir /bin/sh -c 'pwd > "$1"' sh "$tmpdir/pwdout" ';'
out=$(cat "$tmpdir/pwdout")
expected="$tmpdir/root/sub"
if [[ "$out" != "$expected" ]]; then echo "-execdir did not run command in containing directory" >&2; exit 1; fi
","exercises":"-execdir command ; action","expected":"command observes working directory as matched file's directory","expected_to_fail":false},{"filename":"030_delete_nonempty_directory_errors.sh","body":"#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
tmpdir=$(mktemp -d)
trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT
mkdir -p "$tmpdir/nonempty"
: > "$tmpdir/nonempty/child"
set +e
"$UTIL" "$tmpdir/nonempty" -maxdepth 0 -delete > "$tmpdir/out" 2> "$tmpdir/err"
status=$?
set -e
if [[ $status -eq 0 ]]; then echo "-delete succeeded on non-empty directory" >&2; exit 1; fi
","exercises":"-delete fails to remove non-empty directory","expected":"find exits nonzero","expected_to_fail":true}]}
